questions.question: What is the key advantage of rare-earth ion quantum memories?

options.text: Hours-long coherence time and telecom wavelength compatibility

questions.explanation: Rare-earth ion memories (like erbium in crystals) offer extraordinarily long coherence times (up to hours) and operate at 1550nm telecom wavelength, making them ideal for fiber networks despite requiring cryogenic temperatures.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of on-demand single photon sources like quantum dots?

options.text: They emit exactly one photon when triggered (deterministic emission)

questions.explanation: On-demand sources emit a single photon each time triggered, unlike probabilistic sources (like SPDC) that sometimes emit 0, 1, or 2 photons. This deterministic emission greatly simplifies quantum protocols.

questions.question: What does Twin-Field QKD achieve compared to traditional QKD?

options.text: Overcomes the rate-distance limit for longer range without repeaters

questions.explanation: Twin-Field QKD beats the PLOB bound (fundamental rate-distance limit) by measuring interference at a middle station. This enables much longer distances (600+ km demonstrated) without quantum repeaters.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of all-photonic quantum repeaters?

options.text: No quantum memory needed - potentially room temperature operation

questions.explanation: All-photonic repeaters use photonic graph states instead of matter-based quantum memories, potentially enabling room-temperature operation without cryogenics. However, they require many photon sources and careful multiplexing.

questions.question: What efficiency have superconducting nanowire detectors (SNSPDs) achieved?
questions.explanation: SNSPDs have achieved >95% detection efficiency with timing jitter <10ps and near-zero dark counts. Their main limitation is the requirement for cryogenic operation (~2K).

questions.question: What is the advantage of multiplexed photon sources?

options.text: Combining multiple probabilistic sources for near-deterministic output

questions.explanation: Multiplexing combines outputs from multiple probabilistic sources (spatial or temporal) using active switching. When any source succeeds, that photon is routed to output, achieving near-deterministic effective rates.

questions.question: Continuous-variable QKD uses standard telecom components and can operate at room temperature.
questions.explanation: True. CV-QKD uses homodyne/heterodyne detection with standard telecom components, enabling room-temperature operation and high clock rates. However, it typically has shorter range than discrete-variable QKD.
